assistant_message.reasoning:
We need to evaluate the remediation described. The script adds nosuid to /home mount options. That is generally safe. Potential side effects: any setuid binaries under /home would lose privileges, could break applications. However unlikely to affect core services like sshd (which runs from /usr/sbin). So safe.

The script includes platform guard which may be overly strict but it just prevents running; not harmful.

Potential risk: If /home is not separate FS, script aborts; no issue.

Backup and verification.

Thus safe = true.

Side effects: Potential breakage of user-installed setuid binaries in /home. Also possible drift with configuration management.

Services affected: none directly, maybe user login sessions that rely on setuid binaries in /home; but core services unaffected.

Recommendation: Approve (maybe with note to audit setuid files). So "Approve".
